timeline_html:
Summary. Teresa has experienced ongoing shedding for more than a year, which she links to longstanding emotional strain related to relationship difficulties. Her shedding has continued to worsen rather than settle.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Chronic emotional stress from relationship problems (ongoing; started Nov 2017)

Timeline forecast. Because this stress has been persistent, shedding likely rose gradually rather than in a single sharp episode. If emotional balance improves or stress load eases, stabilization typically begins within 1–2 months, with early regrowth appearing another 2–3 months later. Dominant driver: chronic emotional stress, ongoing since late 2017, aligning with the continuous pattern of shedding Teresa reports; this may delay recovery until stress levels improve.

Where you are now. You’re in the stabilization phase—your follicles are still sensitive from prolonged stress, but once emotional load lightens, shedding can ease and baby hairs may start to show within a few months. It takes patience, but this phase does pass. 🌱
